summary: K2.5 closes with Option A: rename the MCP server alias from spec_kit_memory to mk-memory and keep all 59 raw tool names unchanged. This removes Gemini underscore ambiguity with low runtime risk and limits migration to the 166 host-rendered callsites counted in K2.4. Option B, dropping memory_ from raw tool names, is viable only as a later packet after the server-only migration is proven.

claim: Server-only rename from spec_kit_memory to mk-memory is the recommended first migration because it removes Gemini server-alias ambiguity, preserves raw tool-name stability, saves six characters per host-rendered memory_context call, and limits churn to the 166 K2.4 callsites.
evidence: iteration-001.md:14 and iteration-001.md:49 record 166 mcp__mk_spec_memory__ occurrences
iteration-008.md K2.2 recommends mk-memory over underscore aliases
iteration-008.md K2.3 recommends keeping raw tool names unchanged

claim: Dropping memory_ prefixes should be deferred because iteration 8 found the live surface has 59 registrations and only the memory family is partly redundant; other families need prefixes to keep generic verbs intelligible under a mixed server.
evidence: iteration-008.md Part B states TOOL_DEFINITIONS aggregates 59 registrations
iteration-008.md Part B lists graph, session, advisor, task, eval, council, and deep-loop prefix families

claim: The Track-1 ports compose into five downstream packets: 028 memoization plus chunk fingerprints, 029 causal lifecycle tombstones, 030 statediff reconciliation, 031 deterministic causal-edge derivation, and 032 MCP namespace rename.
